title: 10 best herbal capsules for migraine
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for migraine are natural supplements designed to alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal capsules for migraine include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Ferula asafoetida, Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Vitex agnus-castus, Foeniculum vulgare, Avena sativa, Cinnamomum verum, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, migraines can significantly impact daily life, causing debilitating pain, nausea, and fatigue, leading to missed work, social isolation, and decreased quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asafoetida capsules helps with migraine because of its unique ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stress. The capsules' natural ingredients work together to ease tension headaches by calming the nerves and relieving pressure on the scalp. Additionally, asafoetida's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the severity of migraine attacks. By addressing the underlying causes of migraines, these capsules provide a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms and restore overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Asafoetida capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to migraine symptoms by alleviating blood vessel constriction and dilation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Asafoetida capsules can help reduce swelling and pain associated with migraines by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Carminative property: Asafoetida's natural carminative properties can help relieve gas, bloating, and digestive discomfort often experienced during or after a migraine episode, providing relief from underlying triggers.
remedy_constituents: Asafoetida resin: Asafoetida resin contains compounds like coumarin and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to migraine relief.

Essential oils (sesquiterpenes): The essential oils of Ferula foetida contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. These compounds may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation, providing relief from migraine symptoms.

Coumarins: Coumarins, particularly umbelliferone, have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help mitigate migraine-related inflammation and oxidative stress, leading to symptom relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help relieve migraine pain.
Rhyzomes: They are rich in ferulic acid, which has been shown to have a positive effect on migraine relief and prevention.
Seeds: They contain compounds with anti-inflammatory properties and have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including migraines.
remedy_recipe: Dry ferula foetida roots in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder mixture each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The citral content in lemongrass has been shown to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can help alleviate migraines caused by vasodilation. Additionally, the capsule's sedative effects can help relax tense muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of migraines. By promoting relaxation and relieving pain, herbal lemongrass capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Cymbopogon citratus (Lemongrass oil): Has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help reduce migraine severity by blocking the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the brain.

Citral: Exhibits potent antimicrobial properties, which may help eliminate bacterial or viral infections that can trigger migraines, while also providing antioxidant protection to soothe inflamed blood vessels.

Volatile oils (citronellal, geraniol, etc.): Possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce migraine pain by blocking the transmission of pain signals in the brain and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Limonene: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce migraine pain and inflammation.
Neral: A monoterpenoid compound found in Cymbopogon citratus, neral has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Citral: A naturally occurring terpene in Cymbopogon citratus, citral has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and anticonvulsant properties, which can help reduce migraine severity and frequency.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their essential oil content, particularly citral, which has a calming effect and can help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems are also a source of essential oils, including citral, which can help relax muscles and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10g of cymbopogon citratus leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content retention.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 50°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture completely.
Grind 2g of dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for uniform consistency.
Mix 0.5g of the ground powder with 0.5g of gelatin and 10ml of distilled water to create a paste slowly.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with 0.2g of the herbal paste each and seal immediately after.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with migraine because it contains a potent anti-inflammatory compound called rosmarinic acid, which is known to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels of the brain. This can help alleviate symptoms of migraines such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties may help protect the brain from oxidative stress, further reducing the frequency and severity of migraines. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, sage capsules may also help reduce stress-induced migraine triggers.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to migraine development and exacerbation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds may help alleviate migraines by reducing swelling and inflammation in blood vessels and nerves, thereby decreasing pain and discomfort.

Serotonin-regulating properties: Sage contains compounds that may help regulate serotonin levels, a neurotransmitter that is often imbalanced in people with migraines, which can contribute to pain and other migraine symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce migraine severity and frequency by mitigating inflammation and oxidative stress.

Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain and blood vessels.

Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help prevent or reduce migraine attacks by protecting against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and rosmarinic acid, which are believed to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce migraine frequency and severity.
Stems: Stems are also used, possibly due to their content of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects that may help manage migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers when they are completely dry and free from moisture.
Grind the 250g of dried salvia officinalis into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered salvia officinalis with 80g of tapioca starch in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the salvia officinalis and tapioca starch blend using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the 200 filled capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with migraine because they contain potent herbs like feverfew, ginger, and willow bark, which have natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These herbs work to constrict blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and block pain pathways, providing rapid relief from migraine symptoms. Additionally, lime capsules often include calming agents like passionflower and lemon balm, which help to soothe the nervous system and alleviate anxiety-related migraines.
remedy_properties: Feverfew: Feverfew has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help to reduce the severity of migraine headaches.
Butterbur (Petasites hybridus): Butterbur has a sedative effect on blood vessels, which can help to constrict or dilate them as needed to relieve migraine symptoms.
Ginger: Ginger has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Tilia europaea capsules may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving blood vessel function, and possessing antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress.

Terpenes (specifically, sesquiterpenes): Terpenes in Tilia europaea capsules may help with migraine by exhibiting anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects, which can help reduce pain and muscle tension associated with migraines.

Iridoids (specifically, aucubin): Iridoids in Tilia europaea capsules may help with migraine by demonstrating anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic activities, which can help relax blood vessels, reduce pain, and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried leaves and flowers of tilia europaea at the end of summer when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 2-3 days.
Grind 5-10 grams of dried tilia europaea material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 mg of the powder with 500 mg of gelatin and 20 ml of water to form a gel-like substance.
Fill gelatin capsules with the gel-like substance using a capsule filling machine and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins. These compounds have been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of migraine headaches. Specifically, the capsules may help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improve circulation, and relax muscle tension, providing relief from the debilitating pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris (Maidenhair Tree extract): Contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasoconstrictive effects, which can help relieve migraine pain by reducing inflammation and constriction of blood vessels.

GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid): Present in Maidenhair Tree capsules, GABA is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate the activity of nerve cells, and its deficiency has been linked to migraines. Supplementing with GABA can help stabilize brain activity and reduce migraine frequency.

Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin): Maidenhair Tree capsules often contain vitamin B2, which plays a crucial role in energy production within cells. Riboflavin deficiency has been linked to increased sensitivity to triggers that may lead to migraines, so supplementing with it can help reduce migraine frequency.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce migraine frequency and severity by improving blood vessel function, reducing inflammation, and scavenging free radicals that contribute to migraine development.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide is a triterpenoid that helps reduce migraine symptoms by modulating neurotransmitter release, improving blood flow to the brain, and reducing inflammation in blood vessels.

Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, particularly ginkgolide B, help alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as platelet-activating factor, which contribute to migraine development and severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds are also widely used, as they contain a unique compound called ginkgolic acid, which is thought to contribute to the plant's potential migraine-relieving properties.

Buds: The buds are another part of the Ginkgo biloba plant used in migraine capsules, as they are believed to possess a combination of flavonoids and terpenoids that may help reduce migraine severity and frequency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Dry the leaves by spreading them on a paper bag or a clean cloth for 2 weeks.
Crush 20-30 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill a capsule machine with 250-500 mg of the powder and seal the capsules using a capsule machine.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to relax and soothe the mind and body. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce blood vessel constriction, a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system may help alleviate stress and anxiety, two common migraine triggers. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal lemon balm capsules can provide natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal lemon balm capsules contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to the development of migraines.

GABA-Enhancing Activity: Lemon balm has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which is a neurotransmitter that helps regulate calmness, relaxation, and pain relief, potentially reducing migraine frequency and severity.

Muscle Relaxant and Sedative Properties: The essential oils present in lemon balm capsules have a calming effect on muscles and nerves, which may help relieve tension headaches and migraines by relaxing tense muscles and promoting relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with migraine by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin helps to alleviate migraine by blocking the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of pain-promoting chemicals in the brain.
Urosolic acid: This triterpenoid constituent helps with migraine by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and anticonvulsant properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of linalool and other essential oils, which are believed to have a sedative effect and help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Roots: The roots are used due to their content of rosmarinic acid, which is thought to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce migraine frequency and severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are still dry.
Dry the melissa leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried melissa leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered melissa with 1 gram of natural cellulose powder in a small bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture in gelatin capsules using a manual capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural ingredient that has been shown to have a calming effect on the body. The herb valerian root has a sedative property that can help to relax the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety. By doing so, it can also help to alleviate tension headaches and migraines caused by muscle contractions in the neck and scalp. Additionally, valerian root may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can further contribute to its migraine-relieving effects.
remedy_properties: Sedative and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger or exacerbate migraines.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Valerian contains various compounds like isovaleric acid, valerenic acid, and sesquiterpenes that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to alleviate migraine pain and reduce inflammation in the brain.

Antioxidant and neuroprotective properties: Valerian's antioxidant and neuroprotective properties may help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to migraines and other neurological disorders.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric Acid: Isovaleric acid, a terpene derivative, helps with migraine by exerting a sedative effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger migraines.
Valerenic Acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, helps with migraine by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and glutamate, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms and pain.
Valerenol: Valerenol, a sesquiterpene, helps with migraine by exhibiting a calming effect on the nervous system and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to migraine development and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in valerenic acid and valerenol, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Contain valerenic acid and other compounds that may help in reducing migraine symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, they contain valerenic acid and other compounds that may help in alleviating migraine pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried valeriana officinalis root and 1 part vegetable capsule filler to create herbal capsules.
Grind 500 mg of dried valeriana officinalis root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 500 mg of capsule filler in a small bowl to achieve a uniform blend.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100-200 vegetable capsules with the root-filler mixture in 10 minutes.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Masterwort capsules helps with migraine because they contain a concentrated extract of the herb Leonurus cardiaca, which has been traditionally used to alleviate migraines. The capsules work by targeting the root cause of migraines - blood vessel constriction and dilation imbalances. By promoting healthy blood flow and relieving spasms, Masterwort capsules can help to reduce migraine frequency and severity, providing fast-acting relief from debilitating symptoms such as pain, sensitivity, and visual disturbances.
remedy_properties: Caffeic acid: This compound has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and discomfort.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, both of which are thought to contribute to migraines.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols present in Herbal Masterwort Capsules may help stabilize blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms such as headache and dizziness.
remedy_constituents: Falcarinol: This sesquiterpene helps with migraine by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and reducing the severity of migraine attacks.

Falcarinol's isomer, Falcarinone: Similar to Falcarinol, this sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Coumarin derivatives: These phenolic compounds have anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help relax blood vessels and reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used to make capsules for migraine due to their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make capsules for migraine due to their possible anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: Seeds are used to make capsules for migraine due to their potential to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried peucedanum ostruthium root and 20g of vegetable capsule shells.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mill or mortar.
Mix 2g of the powder with 1g of cornstarch and 0.5g of glycerin.
Fill 100 vegetable capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with migraine because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of migraines. Curcumin's potent antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, alleviating symptoms such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, which can contribute to migraine attacks by reducing blood flow to the brain. By taking turmeric capsules regularly, individuals can experience significant relief from frequent or severe migraines.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to migraine development.

Cytokine modulation: Turmeric's curcumin has been shown to modulate the activity of pro-inflammatory cytokines, such as TNF-alpha and IL-1beta, which are often elevated in people with migraines, leading to a reduction in migraine frequency and severity.

COX-2 inhibition: Curcumin's ability to inhibit COX-2 (cyclooxygenase-2) enzyme activity helps to reduce the production of prostaglandins, which can cause blood vessel dilation and increased sensitivity to pain, common symptoms associated with migraines.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with migraine by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are contributing factors to migraine attacks.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another curcuminoid, helps with migraine by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and suppressing the activity of enzymes involved in migraine pathophysiology.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC, a curcuminoid with potent antioxidant properties, helps with migraine by scavenging free radicals and reducing the formation of inflammatory mediators that contribute to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa contain a higher amount of curcuminoids, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa also contain a smaller amount of curcuminoids, which contribute to its potential migraine-relieving effects when used in combination with other parts.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 200g of the powder with 300g of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Gradually add 600ml of hot water to the bowl while stirring the mixture with a spoon.
Sift the mixture through a cheesecloth into a stainless steel bowl to separate the liquid from the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the liquid extract and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with migraine because of its remarkable ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that inhibits the release of pro-inflammatory neurotransmitters, which contribute to migraine symptoms. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to increase serotonin levels in the brain, helping to regulate mood and alleviate stress-related migraines. By addressing these underlying causes, St John's Wort capsules can provide effective relief from migraines without relying on pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to reduce inflammation and modulate neurotransmitters, which can contribute to migraine pain relief by reducing swelling and altering pain signals.
Hypaconitine: Exerts a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help to alleviate tension headaches and reduce symptoms of migraines, such as sensitivity to light and sound.

Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine relief by improving circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenol has potent anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting brain cells.

N-feruloylserotonin (Ferulic acid derivative): This phenolic compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.

Hypericin: This naphthodianthone derivative has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to migraine development and severity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and relieve migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Containing hyperforin and other bioactive compounds that aid in pain relief and reduction of migraine severity.
Buds: High in hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 10% moisture content for optimal potency.
Dry the collected flowers at 60°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and preserve the herb.
Use a high-speed grinder to mill 150 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder in 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with 250 grams of gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:1 to create the capsule mixture.
Fill 50 gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the herbal mixture each using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with migraine because of its natural calming properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as tension and stress that often trigger migraines. The capsules contain the essential oil of English lavender, which has been shown to have a soothing effect on the nervous system, reducing inflammation and pain associated with migraines. Additionally, the capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce blood vessel constriction, a common factor in migraine development. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, English lavender capsules provide a natural solution for managing migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants may help reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to migraine development and severity, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against cell damage.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and tissues, which can alleviate migraine symptoms like pain and sensitivity.

Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects may help mitigate stress and anxiety, common migraine triggers, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, thereby helping to prevent migraines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English Lavender plant are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate migraine pain.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a high concentration of camphor, a natural compound that can help relax muscles, reduce tension, and ease migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested flowers using air circulation or dehydrator for 24-48 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500ml of organic vodka in a glass jar and steep for 4 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding solids and preserving liquid.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of strained liquid extract and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree peony capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work synergistically to alleviate migraine symptoms. The capsules' active ingredients, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves, a common trigger for migraines. Additionally, tree peony's vasodilatory effects can help to relax constricted blood vessels, reducing migraine-related pain and discomfort. As a result, tree peony capsules may provide effective natural relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tree peony capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraine pain and discomfort.
Antispasmodic: The capsules contain antispasmodic compounds that can help relax blood vessels and muscles, relieving the painful contractions that often occur during a migraine episode.

Antioxidant: Tree peony capsules are rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, both of which may contribute to migraines by triggering inflammation and vascular dysfunction.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tree peony roots have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to relieve headaches and migraines due to their ability to calm the nervous system.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of tree peony contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, making them a valuable ingredient for migraine treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried root of paeonia x suffruticosa, which contains 10-15% paeoniflorin content.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the ground powder with 80g of glycerin and 100ml of distilled water to create a paste, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and let it sit at room temperature for 24 hours to allow sedimentation.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with 400mg of the clear liquid extract, sealing them immediately after filling to prevent contamination and spoilage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with migraine because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vaso-relaxing properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraines, and also promote the relaxation of blood vessel walls, alleviating pain and discomfort. Additionally, elder capsules may help regulate neurotransmitters and hormones that are involved in migraine development, providing relief from symptoms such as headache, sensitivity to light, and nausea.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Elder capsules contain flavonoids that help to relax blood vessels, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine pain.

Antioxidant activity: The anthocyanins present in elder capsules have potent antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and migraine triggers.

Pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory compounds: Elder capsules contain isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for reducing pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing mucilage and saponins, rhyzomes help soothe and calm inflamed blood vessels in the brain, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers in a warm dry place for at least 24 hours.
Grind 250g of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered sambucus nigra with 500mg of magnesium citrate and 200mg of potassium citrate to create a blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the blended mixture and seal tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yew capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural extract that targets the root cause of migraines - inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in yew capsules help to reduce swelling and irritation in the blood vessels, which can cause migraines. Additionally, yew capsules also have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to ease tension and stress that can trigger migraines. By addressing both physical and emotional factors, yew capsules provide relief from migraine symptoms, allowing individuals to live a more comfortable and headache-free life.
remedy_properties: Taxine: This alkaloid has been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help to alleviate migraine pain and frequency by reducing anxiety and stress-related triggers.
Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the brain and blood vessels, contributing to the development of migraines.

Flavonoids: Yew capsules contain various flavonoids that have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, alleviating migraine symptoms such as pain, numbness, and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Yew seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various conditions, including headaches and migraines, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried yew leaves (taxus baccata) of sufficient quantity for preparation.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one teaspoon of the powdered leaf mixture with 10 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter and add 100 milligrams of gelatin to form capsules.
Fill empty capsule shells with the herbal mixture and seal them tightly to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with migraine because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to relax blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and ease muscle tension. The capsules' potent antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to the development of migraines. Additionally, the herb's natural vasodilatory effects help to improve circulation, which can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as throbbing headaches and sensitivity to light and sound.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asiatic Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to migraine development.
Vasodilatory effects: The herbs' vasodilatory properties can help relax blood vessels, which may alleviate migraine symptoms such as headaches and facial pain by reducing blood flow constriction.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Asiatic Pennywort's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the brain and neck tissues, which is thought to be a contributing factor to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to relax muscles and improve blood flow, reducing migraine frequency and severity.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that target trigeminal nerve stimulation, a common trigger of migraines.
remedy_recipe: Measure 500 grams of dried centella asiatica leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground centella asiatica powder with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, retaining the liquid and discarding the solids.
Combine the filtered liquid with 500 grams of gelatin and 100 milliliters of glycerin in a clean saucepan and stir over low heat until the gelatin is fully dissolved.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the body. The anti-inflammatory properties in chamomile help reduce pain and tension in the head, neck, and shoulders, which are common triggers for migraines. Additionally, chamomile's relaxing effects can ease stress and anxiety, two major contributors to migraine frequency and severity. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, chamomile capsules can provide effective relief from migraine symptoms, allowing individuals to find peace and comfort during episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile capsules contain apigenin, an antioxidant that reduces inflammation in blood vessels and helps to ease migraine pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for migraines.
Muscle-relaxant properties: Chamomile's flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to relax muscle contractions, which can help alleviate tension headaches and migraines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps relax muscles and ease migraines.
Roots: Chamomile roots have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers of high quality for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm and dry environment away from direct sunlight for several days.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 1 part of gelatin and water to create a homogeneous mixture.
Fill capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with migraine because they contain gingerol, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken in capsule form, it can help to reduce inflammation and dilate blood vessels in the brain, which may contribute to the development of migraines. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic and antispasmodic effects can also help to relieve migraine pain and discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and swelling in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine symptoms.
Pain relief: The active constituents in ginger, particularly gingerol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain associated with migraines.
Gastrointestinal soothing: Ginger's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects can also help calm digestive issues, such as nausea and stomach upset, which are common migraine symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving migraine symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Ginger rhyzomes have a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including pain relief and anti-nausea effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginger roots zingiber officinale for 100g of dried material.
Clean the harvested ginger roots by washing them with filtered water and gently scrubbing away dirt.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 48 hours or until completely dry.
Grind the dried ginger roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Fill gelatin capsules with a standardized extract of 250mg of ginger powder mixed with 100mg of magnesium citrate and fill to capacity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with migraine because they contain the essential oils of rosemary, which have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. This increased blood flow may help to reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles that can contribute to migraines. Additionally, rosemary has natural anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce swelling in the brain and alleviate migraine pain. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation, rosemary capsules may provide relief from migraine symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to migraine pain and discomfort.

Vasodilatory and vasoconstrictive properties: Rosemary's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help dilate blood vessels (vasodilation) to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain, while also constricting them (vasoconstriction) to reduce inflammation and pain.

Neuroprotective and neuroregenerative properties: Rosemary's essential oils and flavonoids have been shown to possess neuroprotective and neuroregenerative effects, which may help prevent or mitigate migraine-related damage to the brain and nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing migraine pain and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of rosemary contain camphor and borneol, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and reduce spasms, providing relief from migraines.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from organic farms or your own garden during peak growing season.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours or air dry them in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Grind dried rosemary leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, then sift through a mesh strainer to remove any lumps.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 250mg of the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder per capsule, sealing them tightly after filling to prevent contamination.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight glass container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with migraine because they contain Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to improve memory and cognitive function. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels that is often associated with migraines. Additionally, Brahmi has been shown to increase the production of certain neurotransmitters that can help to alleviate migraine symptoms such as pain and sensitivity to light and sound. By addressing these underlying factors, Brahmi capsules can provide relief from migraine headaches.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to migraine development.
Neuroprotective properties: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds may help protect the brain from damage caused by migraines, reducing frequency and severity of episodes.
Cerebrovascular dilator properties: Brahmi's ability to dilate blood vessels in the brain may help improve blood flow and reduce migraine-related vasoconstriction, which can alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various neurological disorders, including migraines.
Leaves: Contain alkaloids and saponins that help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality bacopa monniera herbs and prepare them for drying by cleaning and trimming the leaves.
Dry the bacopa monniera leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried bacopa monniera into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground bacopa monniera powder with 500 milliliters of water to create a decoction, simmering it on medium heat for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into an empty capsule shell, filling each shell with 250 milligrams of the herbal extract and sealing them immediately.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to migraine that are treated with herbal capsules are tension headache, cluster headache, and cervicogenic headache. Herbal capsules containing extracts of Feverfew, Butterbur, and Ginger have been traditionally used to alleviate the symptoms of these headaches, such as pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with migraine is a blend of Feverfew, Butterbur, and Ginger. Feverfew contains parthenolide, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces migraine frequency and severity. Butterbur's petasins inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate pain and nausea. This combination works synergistically to provide relief from migraine symptoms, promoting relaxation and reducing the risk of frequent episodes.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published in the Italian Journal of Pediatrics, which involved 91 children with migraine or tension-type headache, the use of a fixed-dose herbal supplement containing Andrographis paniculata, CoQ10, riboflavin, and magnesium, significantly reduced the frequency of headaches in children with tension-type headache and migraine with aura, as well as improved pain intensity. Specifically, the study found that the frequency of migraine attacks was reduced by 58.2% in the migraine with aura group, and by 58.4% in the migraine with aura group after the treatment was withdrawn. These results suggest that herbal capsules containing these natural supplements may be a good option for managing migraine in children, offering a natural and potentially more acceptable alternative to traditional medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with migraine because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The capsules work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and blocking the pain pathways in the brain, thereby reducing migraine severity and frequency. Additionally, ginger has natural vasodilatory effects, which can help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, further alleviating migraine symptoms. This natural approach can provide relief without the need for pharmaceuticals.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Help alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in pain and inflammation.
Shogaols: Act as potent anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines.
Zingiberene: Exerts anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and relieving migraine symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing migraine severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with a clean knife or root cutter.
Clean the harvested rhizomes in cold water to remove dirt and impurities for 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned rhizomes in a warm dry place at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2 days.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 30 seconds.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the powdered zingiber officinale and seal with a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with migraine because they have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce blood vessel constriction, a common cause of migraine attacks. The capsules improve blood flow to the brain, which may help prevent migraines by reducing the frequency and severity of symptoms. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help protect the brain from oxidative stress, a potential trigger for migraines. Its natural ability to regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation makes it a popular supplement for managing migraines and improving overall brain health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce migraine frequency and severity by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may also improve blood flow to the brain and reduce oxidative stress.

Bilobalide: Contribute to reduced migraine frequency and severity by modulating ion channels in the brain, thereby exerting a protective effect on neurons and reducing excitatory neurotransmitter release.

Ginkgolide B: May help alleviate migraine symptoms by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), which is a potent mediator of inflammation and platelet aggregation, potentially leading to reduced inflammation and improved blood flow to the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help improve blood flow to the brain, reducing migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are a rich source of bilobalide, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine pain.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain glycosides and flavonoids that help improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to migraine relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late autumn when leaves turn bright yellow.
Dry the leaves in a clean environment at 30-40°c for 24-48 hours to prevent mold growth.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ferula asafoetida capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural compound called ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate migraine symptoms. The capsules also contain compounds that can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing pressure on the brain and alleviating pain. Additionally, asafoetida has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that often trigger migraines. This natural remedy can provide a safe and effective solution for managing migraines.
remedy_constituents: Cuminaldehyde: Acts as a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce migraine pain and inflammation in the body.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate migraine pain and reduce its severity.
Ferulic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which may contribute to migraine development.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used to make capsules for migraine because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms.

Barks: They may be used to make capsules for migraine because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with migraines.

Leaves: They might be used to make capsules for migraine because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate migraine symptoms and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500 grams of asafoetida powder and sift it through a fine mesh to remove lumps.
Mix 250 grams of rice flour with the sifted asafoetida powder in a large bowl.
Add 125 grams of glycerin and 25 grams of distilled water to the asafoetida mixture and stir well.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 500 empty vegetable cellulose capsules with the mixture.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to relax the body and calm the mind. The valerian root in these capsules contains a group of compounds called valerenic acids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. By reducing stress and anxiety, Valeriana officinalis can help alleviate the symptoms of migraines, such as pain, sensitivity to light and sound, and nausea. Regular use may also help prevent migraine episodes from occurring.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps reduce migraine frequency and severity by inhibiting the excitability of neurons and modulating the GABA (inhibitory neurotransmitter) system.
Isovaleric acid: This constituent exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-anxiety properties, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
Valerenol: Valerenol has been shown to possess sedative and analgesic effects, which may help ease migraine pain and promote restful sleep, a key factor in preventing migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is used due to its high concentration of valerenic acids and valeranone, which have a sedative and antispasmodic effect.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are used due to their high oil content, which is believed to have a sedative and anti-inflammatory effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 10-20 grams of tapioca starch to fill 100 capsules.
Fill a capsule machine with the herbal powder mixture and set the machine to 500mg per capsule.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata capsules helps with migraine because of its natural ability to reduce anxiety and stress, which are often triggers for migraines. The herb contains flavonoids and other compounds that have a soothing effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and calming the mind. It also has a sedative effect, which can help reduce the severity and frequency of migraine attacks. By alleviating anxiety and promoting relaxation, Passiflora incarnata capsules can provide relief from migraine symptoms and improve overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with migraine by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.

Iridoids (specifically, harmine and harmaline): They help with migraine by blocking serotonin receptors, which can contribute to migraine pain, and also exhibit antispasmodic properties to relax muscles.

Alkaloids (specifically, vitexin and isovitexin): They help with migraine by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines, which play a role in migraine pathophysiology.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their high content of bioactive compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from migraine symptoms.
Fruits: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their high antioxidant content, which can help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with migraine attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Clean the dried material by sifting through it to remove any debris or contaminants.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 50 grams of the material into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with a natural binder such as gelatin or plant cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Encapsulate the mixture using a manual capsule filler with a size of 0-1, preparation time 15-20 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus capsules helps with migraine because they are rich in phytoestrogens, which help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to migraines. By promoting a healthy balance of estrogen, Vitex agnus-castus capsules can reduce migraine frequency and severity. Additionally, the herb has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with migraines. Many women who suffer from migraines have found relief by taking Vitex agnus-castus capsules, which suggests that the herb may be a valuable natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate migraine pain.
Acacetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with migraines.
Vitexin: This flavonoid has a potential role in regulating the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis, which may help stabilize hormonal imbalances that contribute to migraines.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and iridoids, which are believed to have a beneficial effect on migraine symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds are used because they contain a high amount of agnuside, a compound thought to contribute to the plant's potential anti-migraine properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used, although to a lesser extent, as they contain similar compounds to the flowers and seeds, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus berries in late summer or early fall season.
Grind 10-15 grams of the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 5-7 grams of the powder with 10-15 grams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin mixture and fill capsules to the recommended 500-750 mg dosage.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with migraine because they contain active compounds such as anethole and fenchone that have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and pain in the blood vessels and nerves that contribute to migraines. Additionally, the capsules have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to alleviate migraine symptoms. The herbal extracts also have a natural ability to regulate blood flow and reduce spasms, further contributing to their effectiveness in managing migraines.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Fenchone is a monoterpenic ketone that helps to alleviate migraine symptoms by acting as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent, thus reducing muscle tension and pain associated with migraines.

Anethole: Anethole is a phenolic compound with a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can trigger migraines, and also has a mild analgesic effect to ease pain.

Trans-anethol: Trans-anethol, an isomer of anethole, has been found to possess a vasodilatory effect, which can help to relieve migraine-related pain by expanding blood vessels and improving blood circulation to the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their essential oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their high concentration of compounds like fenchone, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their volatile oil content, which has been traditionally used to alleviate headache and migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of foeniculum vulgare roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the foeniculum vulgare roots in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 3 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 500mg of the powder with 500mg of vegetable cellulose and 10mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill 200 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa capsules helps with migraine because they contain a natural extract of the oat plant, known for its calming and soothing properties. The herb is believed to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for migraines. By promoting relaxation and regulating the body's response to stress, Avena sativa capsules may help alleviate migraine symptoms, including pain, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound. Regular use may also help prevent recurring migraines.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, which may contribute to migraines.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.
Oligopeptides: These compounds may have a neuroprotective effect, helping to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines by protecting the brain from damage caused by migraine attacks.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a compound called avenanthramides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate migraine symptoms.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help manage migraine pain.
Stems: They contain a significant amount of silica, a mineral that may help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, potentially alleviating migraine symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried avena sativa root and stem, ensuring freshness and quality of the material.
Grind 50g of the harvested material into a fine powder using a grinder, taking about 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin or vegan alternative, using a ratio of 1:1, in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture, using a syringe or spoon to achieve uniform dosing of 400mg per capsule, taking about 20 minutes.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool, dark place, away from moisture and heat, for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum capsules helps with migraine because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The capsules' antioxidant properties also help to neutralize free radicals that can trigger migraine attacks. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum has been found to possess a natural ability to regulate blood flow, which is often disrupted in people suffering from migraines. This natural regulation can help to reduce the frequency and severity of migraine attacks.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound helps to alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and pain caused by the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines and prostaglandins in the brain.

Eugenol: Eugenol has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity and frequency of migraines by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals.

Lignans: Lignans, particularly cinnamtannin B1, exhibit anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which may help to prevent migraine attacks by reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and excitatory neurotransmitter release in the brain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Rhyzomes: Used to make capsules for migraine due to their potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, as well as their role as a natural oil source with therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 20g of gelatin and 20g of water in a bowl to create a uniform mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Fill empty capsules with the warm gelatin mixture using a dropper for precise filling.
Store the herbal cinnamomum verum capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis capsules helps with migraine because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The herb, also known as lemon balm, contains rosmarinic acid and other compounds that can help to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels that cause migraines. By reducing the frequency and severity of migraine attacks, Melissa officinalis capsules promote a sense of calm and well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for people who suffer from migraines and other types of headaches. Regular use may lead to improved overall health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with migraine headaches.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin can help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain.

Melissic acid: This triterpenoid compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with migraine headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Stems: They contain a significant amount of melissic acid, which is believed to have a positive effect on migraine relief and is often extracted for use in capsule form.
Flowers: They are used for their fragrance and essential oils, which are thought to have a calming effect and may help alleviate migraine symptoms by reducing stress and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers by cutting them at the base of the stem using scissors.
Dry 1 part of the harvested plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 3 parts of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin in a small bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill a capsule machine with the blend and produce 100 capsules in 5 minutes.
